components.name: PAvatar
layoutConstraints: Best used as an inline or compact identity element, not as a large profile card.
Status badge and tooltip direction become more important in dense layouts.
accessibilityNotes: Provide a meaningful `name` when the avatar communicates identity so tooltip or fallback text is useful.
Do not rely on badge color alone to communicate status; pair with surrounding text when status meaning matters.
useWhen: Use for single-person identity surfaces such as assignees, owners, or comment authors.
Use when the UI needs a compact identity marker that can degrade from image to initials.
avoidWhen: Avoid as a generic decorative icon; use `PIcon` instead.
Avoid for stacked or overflowed participant sets; use `PAvatarGroup` instead.
components.purpose: Avatar primitive for representing a person, assignee, or participant with image, initials, name, and optional status badge.

components.name: PAvatarGroup
layoutConstraints: Designed for compact horizontal presentation and overflow rather than tall lists.
Works best where a summary of participants is enough and full details live elsewhere.
accessibilityNotes: Do not assume the overlap alone communicates all identities; pair with nearby text when the participant list is critical.
Preserve tooltip or visible names for discoverability in dense group displays.
useWhen: Use for collaborator lists or participant stacks where multiple people need to be represented compactly.
Use when you need a capped visible count with overflow rather than rendering every avatar at full width.
avoidWhen: Avoid when each person needs separate labels or actions inline; render individual `PAvatar` rows instead.
Avoid for non-person entities or arbitrary chips.
components.purpose: Grouped avatar display for teams, participants, or shared ownership with overflow handling.

components.name: PBadge
layoutConstraints: Best for short inline content such as one-word statuses or small counts.
Not intended to serve as a full-width container or large interactive surface.
accessibilityNotes: Do not rely on badge color alone to communicate meaning when status is important.
Keep badge text short so the semantic label remains scannable.
useWhen: Use for short status labels, counts, or state chips that should remain visually lightweight.
Use when you need semantic color treatment without the interaction weight of a button.
avoidWhen: Avoid for primary actions; use `PButton` instead.
Avoid for long-form content or multi-select token displays; use `PChip` or text instead.
components.purpose: Compact status or count indicator for inline emphasis, state labeling, or small metric display.

components.name: PButton
layoutConstraints: Intended for discrete action affordances, not for full-row interactive list items.
Neutral and white variants are more context-sensitive and should be used where surrounding surfaces support them.
accessibilityNotes: Icon-only usage should still expose an accessible name from surrounding context or explicit labeling.
Disabled state should not be the only cue for blocked workflows; pair with explanatory text when needed.
useWhen: Use for the highest-priority action in a section, form, or dialog.
Use the variant and appearance props to encode emphasis rather than creating custom button styles.
avoidWhen: Avoid for passive status display; use `PBadge` or `PTypography` instead.
Avoid for navigation containers or selection menus; use `PMenu`, `PSelectMenu`, or `PSelect` depending on the pattern.
components.purpose: Primary action trigger for standard flows, including form submit, toolbar actions, inline actions, and link-styled actions.

components.name: PCheckbox
layoutConstraints: Best for individual options or row-level selection patterns.
Should remain compact and aligned with adjacent label text.
accessibilityNotes: Always provide a visible or programmatic label for the checkbox meaning.
Treat indeterminate as a summary state, not a hidden third meaning without explanation.
useWhen: Use for independent yes or no options, row selection, or optional settings.
Use when the user may select zero, one, or many independent items.
avoidWhen: Avoid for mutually exclusive choices; use radio-group style selection instead.
Avoid for long lists that need grouped validation or helper messaging; use `PCheckboxGroup`.
components.purpose: Single boolean or tri-state selection control with label support.

components.name: PCheckboxGroup
layoutConstraints: Intended for compact vertical or horizontal option sets rather than extremely long filter panels.
Works best when all options are visible at once.
accessibilityNotes: Group labeling and helper or error messaging should communicate the purpose of the whole set.
Orientation changes layout only; ensure the reading and tab order still makes sense.
useWhen: Use when several related checkbox options belong to one logical form field.
Use when helper text or error text should apply to the entire option set.
avoidWhen: Avoid when options are independent and do not need grouped messaging.
Avoid for dropdown-style multi-select behavior; use `PSelect` or `PSelectMenu` instead.
components.purpose: Grouped checkbox field with shared label, helper text, and error handling for related multi-select options.

components.name: PIcon
layoutConstraints: The Figma icon system is based on a fixed 16px icon size, with component-level sizing only where the host component supports it.
Icons are intended to live inside components rather than act as standalone clickable elements.
accessibilityNotes: Icons should not be the only accessible label for a critical action unless the parent control provides a clear name.
Treat icons as presentational unless the surrounding component intentionally exposes accessible meaning.
useWhen: Use inside existing interactive components such as buttons, menus, search fields, table actions, and navigation items.
Use decorative or supportive icons to improve scanning when the surrounding component already carries the interaction semantics.
avoidWhen: Avoid making bare icons the only affordance for important or ambiguous actions.
Avoid recoloring or resizing icons arbitrarily outside the design-token and supported size system.
components.purpose: System icon primitive for rendering Partner icons first and falling back to the Carbon icon set when a Partner-specific icon does not exist.

components.name: PLayout
layoutConstraints: Designed as a top-level page shell, not a nested layout primitive.
Side panels consume fixed structural space and are best suited to desktop-oriented page composition.
accessibilityNotes: Ensure the content placed in header and panel slots still provides a coherent heading and landmark structure.
Hidden and shown side-panel states should remain discoverable if they affect key functionality.
useWhen: Use for full-page or workspace-level screens that need consistent header and side-panel structure.
Use when the body content should align to the Partner responsive grid tokens.
avoidWhen: Avoid inside cards, popovers, or modal-scale content.
Avoid when you only need a small local two-column section rather than a full page shell.
components.purpose: Page-shell layout primitive for Partner-style pages with optional header, side panels, footer, and grid-based body content.

components.name: PLogo
layoutConstraints: The master Figma file distinguishes full logos from favicon variants, so choose the intended form factor explicitly.
Brand-specific variants should stay consistent with the approved asset list rather than being improvised from text.
accessibilityNotes: Provide an appropriate `alt` value when the logo conveys brand identity or serves as a linked home or product marker.
If the logo is purely decorative, the consuming surface should avoid duplicating the brand name excessively.
useWhen: Use when the UI needs an approved brand mark rather than a text label or generic graphic.
Use for product switching, brand attribution, shell headers, or favicon-like display where the design system defines the specific logo variant.
avoidWhen: Avoid using logos as a substitute for navigation text or accessible labels.
Avoid creating custom brand mark variations when the design system already defines the logo and favicon variants.
components.purpose: Brand-logo primitive for rendering Partner, SiteLynx, BlueLynx, Plink, and related branded logo assets, including favicon treatments.

components.name: PMenu
layoutConstraints: Best for compact overlay content anchored to a trigger.
Not intended to replace full-page filter or navigation panels.
accessibilityNotes: The trigger should expose a clear accessible name that explains what menu will open.
Searchable menus should still preserve keyboard access to items and clear focus movement.
useWhen: Use for action menus or compact dropdown content that is not a form field.
Use when you need a custom trigger and flexible content regions such as header, footer, and searchable items.
avoidWhen: Avoid for standard form-field selection; use `PSelect`.
Avoid when you need the opinionated selectable menu behavior of `PSelectMenu`.
components.purpose: Generic dropdown menu container for action lists, searchable menu content, and custom trigger plus content composition.

components.name: PPopover
layoutConstraints: Sized for contextual overlay content, not page-scale containers.
Direction and offset should be chosen with surrounding layout density in mind.
accessibilityNotes: The trigger should clearly indicate that additional contextual content will open.
Interactive content inside the popover should remain keyboard reachable and closable.
useWhen: Use for richer contextual content than a tooltip, especially when the content may include actions or structured sections.
Use when the trigger should reveal supplementary content without navigating away.
avoidWhen: Avoid for simple hover or focus descriptions; use `PTooltip` instead.
Avoid for full dialog or workflow content that deserves modal treatment.
components.purpose: Popover surface for contextual content, help text, or small interactive content anchored to a trigger.

components.name: PSearchBar
layoutConstraints: Works best as a horizontal control in toolbars, filter bars, or list headers.
Debounce behavior should match the data-fetching or filtering expectations of the surrounding UI.
accessibilityNotes: Placeholder text alone should not be the only explanation of search scope when context is ambiguous.
If the search button is hidden, surrounding context should still make the field purpose obvious.
useWhen: Use for page-level or panel-level search entry where the user searches a collection or list.
Use when both immediate debounced updates and explicit search submission are useful.
avoidWhen: Avoid for generic labeled text input fields; use `PTextInput` instead.
Avoid when the pattern is selection from known options rather than free-text querying.
components.purpose: Search input primitive with debounced value updates and explicit search and clear events.

components.name: PSelect
layoutConstraints: Designed as a form control, so it fits best in forms, filter bars, and settings panels rather than generic tool menus.
Chip variant is useful when selected items need to stay visible after selection.
accessibilityNotes: Use the `label`, `helperText`, and `errorText` props to provide a complete accessible field description.
Searchable and multi-select states should still preserve understandable trigger text and selection feedback.
useWhen: Use as a labeled form input when the selected value belongs in form state.
Use for single or multi-select fields that need helper text, validation, or chip-style selected-value display.
avoidWhen: Avoid for lightweight action menus; use `PMenu`.
Avoid when the trigger-driven filter menu pattern is primary and form-field chrome is unnecessary; use `PSelectMenu`.
components.purpose: Form-field select control for single or multi-selection with label, helper text, error states, and optional searchable menu behavior.

components.name: PSelectMenu
layoutConstraints: Best for toolbar and filter contexts where the trigger owns the overlay.
The consumer typically controls trigger width, so avoid assuming field-like full-width layout.
accessibilityNotes: The trigger should describe the menu purpose, not just display a generic icon or ambiguous label.
Multi-select behavior should keep selected-state feedback understandable for keyboard users.
useWhen: Use for filter menus and compact trigger-driven selection workflows.
Use when selection should happen from a menu surface rather than a full form field.
avoidWhen: Avoid when the control needs a standard field label, helper text, or error text; use `PSelect`.
Avoid for generic action menus without selectable state; use `PMenu`.
components.purpose: Trigger-based selectable menu for single or multi-select overlay interactions with apply, clear, and search behaviors.

components.name: PTable
layoutConstraints: Use the table subcomponents within semantic table structure rather than mixing them into arbitrary div layouts.
Wide content should be handled intentionally with truncation, scrolling, or supporting layout decisions.
accessibilityNotes: Keep header and cell relationships semantically correct so assistive technology can interpret the grid.
Sortable states should remain visible and understandable beyond icon treatment alone.
useWhen: Use for structured row and column data where headers, cells, and scanning alignment matter.
Use when the table needs empty, caption, footer, or sortable-header states.
avoidWhen: Avoid for card-based lists or highly responsive stacked mobile layouts that no longer behave like tables.
Avoid when the content is mostly form controls and not tabular data.
components.purpose: Semantic table container and companion subcomponents for tabular data, sortable headers, captions, footers, and empty states.

components.name: PTextArea
layoutConstraints: Best for form sections with enough vertical space to support multiple lines.
Textarea growth and resize behavior should align with surrounding form density.
accessibilityNotes: Use label and helper or error text to explain expected content and constraints.
If max length matters, expose that information visibly and not only in validation after input.
useWhen: Use for longer freeform text such as notes, descriptions, or comments.
Use when a field needs the same form-field affordances as `PTextInput` but with multi-line entry.
avoidWhen: Avoid for short single-line inputs such as names, email, or compact filters; use `PTextInput`.
Avoid when the content is a fixed selection rather than authored text.
components.purpose: Multi-line text field with label, helper text, error messaging, and optional character or sizing constraints.

components.name: PTextInput
layoutConstraints: Intended as a form control and usually works best at field or panel width rather than as bare inline text.
Short helper or error text fits best directly below the field.
accessibilityNotes: Prefer the built-in `label`, `helperText`, and `errorText` props over relying only on placeholder text.
Use the typed variants only when they reflect actual input semantics.
useWhen: Use for standard form entry such as names, emails, URLs, passwords, and short freeform values.
Use when the field needs built-in validation messaging or helper text.
avoidWhen: Avoid for search interactions; use `PSearchBar`.
Avoid for multi-line authored text; use `PTextArea`.
components.purpose: Single-line text field with built-in label, helper text, error text, and typed input variants such as email or password.

components.name: PTooltip
layoutConstraints: Designed for short text content and compact spatial footprint.
Direction should be chosen to avoid clipping in dense interfaces.
accessibilityNotes: Tooltip content should be supplemental rather than the sole source of required information.
Triggers still need meaningful labels even if a tooltip is present.
useWhen: Use for short non-essential clarification on hover or focus.
Use when an inline label would add too much visual noise but the user still benefits from a concise explanation.
avoidWhen: Avoid for interactive or multi-section content; use `PPopover`.
Avoid as the only way to expose critical instructions.
components.purpose: Lightweight tooltip for brief contextual help or short explanatory text attached to a trigger.

components.name: PTypography
layoutConstraints: Designed as a text primitive and should usually remain inside broader layout or form components.
Variant and semantic element can differ, but that should be intentional.
accessibilityNotes: Choose the `component` prop to preserve semantic heading and text structure, not just visual appearance.
Heading hierarchy should remain logical across the page regardless of visual size choices.
useWhen: Use when Partner type-scale consistency matters across headings, body text, labels, helper text, and button-like text.
Use to express text hierarchy without inventing one-off class stacks.
avoidWhen: Avoid wrapping text only to recreate semantics already provided by another component's built-in label or message props.
Avoid using typography variants as a substitute for interactive components.
components.purpose: Typography primitive that maps Partner type-scale variants to semantic HTML elements and alignment options.
